steps.title: Recognize the restatement
steps.content: The phrase after the blank rephrases what comes before it—a concept is stated in technical language and then restated in plainer terms. The transition must mark this clarification.

steps.title: Match the clarification signal
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification/restatement — a technical concept is stated and then rephrased in plainer terms. The blank needs a transition signaling clarification.
  • Choice A — ‘likewise,’: signals similarity between two separate things, so fails — only one concept is being expressed, not two comparable ones. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘that is,’: signals a restatement or clarification of what just preceded it, so fits. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘in addition,’: signals a new, separate point, so fails — the second clause is not adding new information but restating the same idea. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘for example,’: signals a specific instance of a general claim, so fails — no example is being introduced. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
